title: The One On The Last Night

jokes.text: When's it due? -On the first. And that's every month? No, just the months you want to live here.
jokes.setup: Joey asks if rent is due every month with genuine confusion
jokes.punchline: Monica's sarcastic response about only paying when you want to live there
jokes.explanation: Clean setup/punchline with Monica's dry delivery contrasting Joey's naivety. Good economy and character-specific humor.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait, rent is due every month?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Yes, Joey. Every month. It's kind of a requirement if you want to keep living here.

jokes.text: Oh, my God! That's our phone number.
jokes.setup: Joey sees the phone bill amount
jokes.punchline: He thinks the bill total is their phone number
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - his specific brand of confusion. Economical and character-specific stupidity that feels authentic to him.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa! 555-1247? That's our phone number!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Joey, that's the bill.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I know.

jokes.text: Joey Tribbiani does not take charity... anymore.
jokes.setup: Joey declares he doesn't take charity with pride
jokes.punchline: The pause and 'anymore' reveals his history of taking charity
jokes.explanation: Good use of pause for comedic timing. The 'anymore' deflates his dignified stance perfectly.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I don't take charity.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I have my pride.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: ...anymore.

jokes.text: Why don't you hire him as an actor? You could have him dress up and put on skits whenever you want.
jokes.setup: Need to give Joey money without hurting his pride
jokes.punchline: Rachel suggests hiring him to dress up and perform skits
jokes.explanation: Absurd solution that treats Joey like a personal entertainer. Funny concept but not deeply character-specific.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: We could hire him to dress up and perform skits.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Rachel, that's actually not a terrible idea.

jokes.text: Well, that would help the pride thing.
jokes.setup: Rachel's absurd suggestion about hiring Joey for skits
jokes.punchline: Monica's dry acknowledgment that this would indeed help his pride
jokes.explanation: Monica's sarcastic response works but isn't particularly memorable or clever.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Yeah, because nothing says 'I believe in you' like giving you a job you're not qualified for.

jokes.text: And also my birthday. -It's not your birthday. What a mean thing to say! I'd never tell you it wasn't your birthday.
jokes.setup: Rachel claims it's also her birthday
jokes.punchline: When corrected, she acts hurt and claims she'd never be so mean
jokes.explanation: Rachel's deflection technique is character-appropriate and gets a decent laugh through false indignation.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: It's my birthday too!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No it's not.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Well, I would never be so mean to you on your birthday!

jokes.text: Not if you talk about how you gave up basketball to become a paleontologist.
jokes.setup: Ross invites Joey to watch basketball
jokes.punchline: Joey's preemptive strike about Ross's predictable story
jokes.explanation: Good character callback - Joey knows Ross will tell this story again. Shows their relationship history.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey Ross, I'd love to watch the game with you.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Great! It'll be fun. You know, this reminds me of the time in 1987 when I went to a Knicks game and...

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: And you wore the same sweater three days in a row because you thought it was lucky?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well, yes, but how did you—

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You tell this story every time we watch basketball, man.

jokes.text: I did give up a career in basketball to become a paleontologist!
jokes.setup: Joey preemptively complains about Ross telling his basketball story
jokes.punchline: Ross immediately proves Joey right by telling the story anyway
jokes.explanation: Ross's inability to resist telling his story despite Joey's warning is perfect character work - he can't help himself.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Here we go...

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: So there I was, right? And I'm dribbling the ball down the court, and I look up, and I see this opening...

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh my God.

jokes.text: And clearly, not a minute sooner.
jokes.setup: Phoebe says they're supposed to start having fun in 15 minutes
jokes.punchline: Monica's rigid response about the exact timing
jokes.explanation: Classic Monica control freak moment - her need for precise scheduling even for fun is perfectly in character.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: We're supposed to start having fun in 15 minutes.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: We are? Well, I better start getting ready now.

jokes.text: What you're wearing is fine for that.
jokes.setup: Monica sees Rachel hasn't packed and will likely be killed by Monica
jokes.punchline: Monica's matter-of-fact assessment that Rachel's clothes are appropriate for being murdered
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered with Monica's characteristic deadpan style. Great economy and unexpected response.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, at least you're dressed for it.

jokes.text: Surprise! Don't get mad, because this is what happened. I started packing, then I realized, "What am I doing?" I am lousy at packing, right? But you love packing! So as a gift to you on our last night... I'll be coordinator!
jokes.setup: Rachel hasn't packed and Monica is about to explode
jokes.punchline: Rachel spins her laziness as a gift to Monica who loves packing
jokes.explanation: Rachel's masterful manipulation and spin is perfectly in character. Long setup but the reframe payoff works well.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Rachel, we're leaving in an hour! Why aren't you packed?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I know, I know, I've just been so busy...

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Busy doing what?!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Well, think about it. You LOVE packing. You love organizing, making lists, folding things perfectly. So really, I'm doing you a favor by not packing. You get to do what you love!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That is not a favor, that is insane!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: You're welcome.

jokes.text: Oh, my God, I'm so sorry. I didn't get you anything.
jokes.setup: Rachel presents not packing as a gift to Monica
jokes.punchline: Monica sarcastically apologizes for not getting Rachel a gift in return
jokes.explanation: Monica's sarcastic response to Rachel's ridiculous gift logic. Good character beat but not exceptional.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh, well I'm sorry I didn't get you a gift in return.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I would have, but I was too busy packing.

jokes.text: My God, thank you! Monica's gonna make you pack! She's got jobs for everyone! Now, it's too late for me, but save yourselves!
jokes.setup: Rachel dramatically warns the guys about Monica's packing army
jokes.punchline: Her over-the-top war movie parody delivery
jokes.explanation: Rachel's dramatic warning works as physical comedy but isn't particularly original or character-specific.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Okay, listen to me. Monica has an army. They're coming. They're organized, they're efficient, and they will pack everything you own into labeled boxes whether you like it or not.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I've seen things. Bubble wrap. So much bubble wrap. She's color-coded the labels by room. BY ROOM!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: This isn't a move anymore, guys. This is a military operation. And we are the enemy.

jokes.text: I have plans with Joey. -You said you were doing nothing. -Yes, but for the last time.
jokes.setup: Monica points out Chandler said he was doing nothing
jokes.punchline: Chandler clarifies he's doing nothing, but specifically for the last time with Joey
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that reframes 'doing nothing' as a specific activity. Good Chandler logic and timing.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You said you were doing nothing!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I am doing nothing. But specifically for the last time with Joey.

jokes.text: Because I have Ben! It's almost past his bedtime. Where is he? He's at a dinner party.
jokes.setup: Ross uses Ben as an excuse not to help pack
jokes.punchline: When asked where Ben is, he reveals Ben is at a dinner party - not with Ross
jokes.explanation: Ross getting caught in his own excuse is good character comedy - his immediate contradiction is funny.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I can't help you pack, I have to watch Ben.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Where is Ben?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: He's at a dinner party.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: A dinner party? I thought you were watching him!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I am watching him... from here.

jokes.text: I gotta go make a fake Ben.
jokes.setup: Ross realizes he needs Ben to maintain his excuse
jokes.punchline: His matter-of-fact statement about making a fake Ben
jokes.explanation: Absurd but logical next step for Ross. The casualness of 'making a fake Ben' is perfectly delivered.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I could always just make another Ben.

jokes.text: Our last dinner together. Me, bringing the food of my ancestors... you, the food of yours!
jokes.setup: Chandler ordered pizza, Joey ordered Chinese for their last night
jokes.punchline: Chandler's mock-serious characterization of pizza and Chinese as ancestral foods
jokes.explanation: Chandler's sarcastic take on their food choices is mildly amusing but not exceptional.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Pizza and Chinese food... they're not just meals, they're a legacy. They're what our fathers ate, and their fathers before them.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Chandler, that's beautiful, man.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I know. I'm thinking of having it bronzed.

jokes.text: Are you crazy? You haven't beaten me since my injury-plagued '97 season.
jokes.setup: Chandler suggests playing foosball for money
jokes.punchline: Joey refers to his foosball history like a professional athlete
jokes.explanation: Joey treating foosball like professional sports with an 'injury-plagued season' is perfectly absurd and character-appropriate. Very quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh, you wanna play for money? Alright, but I gotta warn you - I was the foosball champion at my high school for three years running. I had a scholarship offer from a junior college. Had to turn it down because of my acting career, but let me tell you, my wrist action is still sharp.

jokes.text: What do you say to $50? I say to it: "50 dollars, you are more than I have."
jokes.setup: Chandler suggests betting $50
jokes.punchline: Joey literally addresses the money amount as if it's a person
jokes.explanation: Joey's literal interpretation of 'what do you say to $50' is clever wordplay that's perfectly in character for his literal mindset.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'll bet you $50 you can't go a week without saying something stupid.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You're on! Fifty dollars, you are going down.

jokes.text: The big game. Italy versus... China, apparently.
jokes.setup: Joey and Chandler are about to play foosball with pizza and Chinese food nearby
jokes.punchline: Joey frames it as Italy vs China based on their food choices
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to the food conversation but not particularly clever or memorable.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Okay, I'm Italy.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: You're Italy? Why are you Italy?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Because I got the pizza! You're China.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'm China?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, you got the Chinese food!

jokes.text: I guess you weren't there.
jokes.setup: Rachel starts reminiscing about getting rollerblades with Monica
jokes.punchline: Awkward realization that Monica wasn't actually there for the memory
jokes.explanation: Awkward beat that creates uncomfortable silence but isn't particularly strong or memorable.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, remember when we got those rollerblades? That was so fun!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Rachel, I wasn't there.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: What? Yes you were!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No, I really wasn't. That was you and Phoebe.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God...

jokes.text: She's perfect.
jokes.setup: Phoebe asks Monica if there's anything she won't miss about Rachel
jokes.punchline: Monica's deadpan assertion that Rachel is perfect
jokes.explanation: Simple deadpan delivery but lacks originality or strong character insight.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Rachel is perfect. I won't miss a thing.

jokes.text: I'm not gonna miss the fact that you're never allowed to move the phone pen.
jokes.setup: Monica reluctantly admits something she won't miss about Rachel
jokes.punchline: Her hyper-specific control issue about the phone pen location
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Monica's obsessive control issues in a relatable, specific way. Great character integration.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Okay, fine. There is one thing I won't miss about Rachel.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You always moving the pen that's attached to the phone.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I do not!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Yes, you do. It's supposed to be on the right side of the phone. You move it to the left, or sometimes you even take it off completely!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, Monica...

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I'm not gonna miss that.

jokes.text: Not just the phone pen. I never get my messages. You get your messages. It doesn't count if you have to read them off your hand after you've fallen asleep on the couch.
jokes.setup: Monica's complaint about the phone pen opens floodgates
jokes.punchline: Specific image of Rachel reading messages off her hand after sleeping on couch
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with specific visual that feels authentic to Rachel's scattered lifestyle.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Why does nobody ever return my phone pen?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Because nobody wants to write with a pen that's been in your hair.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What? That's ridiculous!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Actually, it's not. I once found a Cheeto in there.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: That was me. I was looking for snacks.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Besides, Monica, last week I fell asleep on the couch and woke up with 'Call Joanne' written backwards on my face.

jokes.text: So you missed a message from who? Chandler? Or your mom? Or Chandler? Or your mom?
jokes.setup: Rachel defends herself about missing messages
jokes.punchline: Her repetition reveals Monica's limited social circle
jokes.explanation: Rachel's counter-attack pointing out Monica's limited social circle is mildly effective character comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I didn't get your messages!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I called you three times!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Well I didn't get them.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I called you three times!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Well I didn't get them.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I called you three times!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: In her defense, that was the extent of Monica's weekend calls.

jokes.text: It worked! No one's sad!
jokes.setup: Phoebe's plan was to help them focus on things they won't miss
jokes.punchline: Her cheerful observation as Monica and Rachel start arguing instead of being sad
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's oblivious pride in her 'success' creates ironic humor but isn't particularly strong.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: See? You're already fighting! You won't even miss each other!

jokes.text: That's, like, 500 bucks you owe me! Man! Five hundred dollars. That is a lot of electricity, huh?
jokes.setup: Chandler has won $500 from Joey at foosball
jokes.punchline: Joey's attempt to put the loss in perspective by comparing it to their electric bill
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the earlier electric bill joke, showing Joey trying to rationalize his loss in familiar terms.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Five hundred dollars? That's like... what, three months of electricity?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Joey, our electric bill is like thirty bucks a month.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, I don't really pay attention to those things.

jokes.text: No... one can beat me!
jokes.setup: Joey is dominating at foosball and getting overconfident
jokes.punchline: His dramatic declaration followed by immediately breaking the foosball table
jokes.explanation: Classic physical comedy setup - Joey's hubris immediately punished. Good timing and comeuppance.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I am a foosball god! Nothing can stop me now!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I am UNSTOPPABLE!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait... what was that sound?

jokes.text: That's why only the little fake men are supposed to do the kicking.
jokes.setup: Joey has just broken the foosball table with his enthusiasm
jokes.punchline: Chandler's dry explanation of proper foosball technique
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler response - dry, logical explanation delivered deadpan after physical comedy moment.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: You know, Joey, foosball is a game of finesse, not brute strength. You don't need to treat it like you're arm-wrestling a bear. The key is subtle wrist rotation and a light touch on the handles.

jokes.text: Oh, your brother. Score!
jokes.setup: Ross calls Monica
jokes.punchline: Joey celebrates that someone other than Chandler or Monica's mom is calling
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier social circle joke but Joey's excitement about Ross calling is mildly funny.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Hey Monica, it's Ross.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yes! Finally! Someone other than Chandler or Monica's mom is calling!

jokes.text: I guess he doesn't feel like talking right now. He's smiling, though.
jokes.setup: Ross is pretending to have Ben with him on the phone
jokes.punchline: His awkward description of 'Ben's' behavior reveals he's looking at something inanimate
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of the fake Ben plot with Ross's awkward improvisation revealing the deception.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Hey buddy, it's Dad. Yeah, I know, I know, it's been a while. So what have you been up to? Uh-huh. Oh really? That's great. Well, right now I'm just sitting here looking at you... I mean, talking to you on the phone.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: You're not moving much today, are you? Just kind of... sitting there. Very still.

jokes.text: You know, Daddy was gonna be a basketball player.
jokes.setup: Ross is 'talking' to his fake Ben
jokes.punchline: He tells his basketball story to an inanimate object
jokes.explanation: Ross can't help telling his basketball story even to a fake Ben. Good character consistency but predictable.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: So there I was, right? Senior year of high school. I'm on the basketball court, and I'm about to shoot the winning basket...

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: And then... nothing. Because you're a dinosaur, Ben. You don't care about basketball.

jokes.text: I'm Monica. I wash the toilets 17 times a day, even if people are on it!
jokes.setup: Rachel and Monica's fight escalates to impressions
jokes.punchline: Rachel's exaggerated impression of Monica's cleaning obsession
jokes.explanation: Good Monica impression that captures her cleaning obsession with absurd escalation (cleaning while people are using toilet).

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh, I'm Monica Geller. I have to organize my shoes by color AND season. My apartment is so clean, I could perform surgery in here. Actually, I probably have!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That is NOT what I sound like!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Yes it is. And excuse me while I go alphabetize my spice rack... by cuisine AND heat level.

jokes.text: Hi, I'm Rachel. Is my sweater too tight? I'd better wash it and shrink it!
jokes.setup: Monica retaliates with her own impression
jokes.punchline: Mocking Rachel's fashion choices and laundry incompetence
jokes.explanation: Monica's comeback impression targeting Rachel's vanity and domestic incompetence. Decent but not as strong as Rachel's.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh, you want an impression? Okay. 'Oh my God, I'm Rachel Green, I'm so pretty and sophisticated, but I can't do my own laundry because I'm an idiot. I just throw everything in together and ruin it all.'

jokes.text: Oh, my God! I love Ross. I hate Ross. I love Ross. I hate Ross!
jokes.setup: Monica continues her Rachel impression
jokes.punchline: Mocking Rachel's on-and-off relationship with Ross
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Rachel's indecisive feelings about Ross. Captures her pattern well with economical delivery.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God, I'm Rachel Green and I can't decide if I love Ross or not. Should I be with him? Should I not? Oh, the drama of it all!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Maybe I'll get back together with him. No wait, maybe I won't. Actually, you know what? I will. No, I won't.

jokes.text: My God! I can't find a boyfriend. I'll stumble across the hall and sleep with the first guy I find there!
jokes.setup: Monica's impression continues attacking Rachel's romantic life
jokes.punchline: Harsh characterization of how Rachel ended up with Joey
jokes.explanation: Monica's impression gets meaner, referencing Rachel's desperation. Dark but effective character attack.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: And that's how you ended up with Joey — because apparently, hitting rock bottom romantically means dating the guy who thinks a menu is a novel.

jokes.text: Boo-hoo. Get out of my room!
jokes.setup: Phoebe tries to remind them this is their last night together
jokes.punchline: Monica's childish dismissal and claim of ownership
jokes.explanation: Monica's petulant response shows her regression to childish behavior. Economical but not particularly clever.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: This is our last night together!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No it's not. I'm keeping you all.

jokes.text: There are other ways to win back your money. How about blackjack? Not my game.
jokes.setup: Chandler suggests blackjack to help Joey win back money
jokes.punchline: Joey immediately shuts it down as not his game
jokes.explanation: Simple setup for Chandler to introduce the fake card game. Functional but unremarkable.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Why don't you play some blackjack? You could win back some money.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: No, no, no. Blackjack's not my game.

jokes.text: Cups. -I don't know how. -I'll teach you. It's easy and fun.
jokes.setup: Chandler needs to create a fake game to give Joey money
jokes.punchline: He invents 'Cups' with confident enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the elaborate scheme. Chandler's commitment to the fake game name 'Cups' is absurd and endearing.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Okay, I got it. I'm gonna create a game.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: A game?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, it's called 'Cups.' You flip cups over, and if you guess which one has the ball under it, you win money.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: That's genius!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I know, right? I'm thinking we could make a lot of money off this thing.

jokes.text: I have two queens. What do you have? -A 2 and a 5. -You win! Fifty dollars!
jokes.setup: First hand of the made-up card game 'Cups'
jokes.punchline: Joey wins with clearly worse cards according to any real game
jokes.explanation: Good demonstration of Chandler's elaborate scheme with Joey's innocent acceptance. Sets up the running gag well.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Okay, so you have... two sevens and a king?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: And I have three aces.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I win!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What? How do you win with two sevens and a king?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: That's a really good hand in Cups.

jokes.text: That's a full cup! Damn, I am good at Cups!
jokes.setup: Joey gets 4 and 9, Chandler acts shocked
jokes.punchline: Joey's pride in his 'full cup' and natural talent at the fake game
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - his genuine pride in being good at a completely made-up game. Great character comedy with quotable delivery.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Wait, you got a 4 and a 9?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah! That's 13!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: That's... that's actually pretty good.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I know! And look at my cup - it's completely full. I'm a natural at this game.

jokes.text: She's not crying. She's holding it in. She's so brave. Go.
jokes.setup: Chandler says he can hear Monica crying
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's dramatic interpretation of Monica's 'brave' silence
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's dramatic interpretation is mildly funny but not particularly strong or memorable.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: She's not crying, she's just being brave. It's like when a soldier comes home from war and doesn't talk about the horrible things he's seen. Monica's a silent hero.

jokes.text: It needs to go about 20 blocks to the left.
jokes.setup: Monica asks if a picture is straight
jokes.punchline: Rachel's extreme exaggeration about how far off it is
jokes.explanation: Rachel's sarcastic exaggeration about the picture placement is mildly amusing but not exceptional.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Is this picture straight?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Straight? Monica, it's so crooked, I think it's trying to parallel park!

jokes.text: Great. Monica's moving. -I am not! -Really? How come all your stuff is in this box?
jokes.setup: Monica claims she's not moving while Rachel unpacks
jokes.punchline: Chandler points out Monica's stuff is already packed
jokes.explanation: Chandler's logical observation cutting through their argument. Functional but not particularly clever.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I'm not moving.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, and all your stuff is already packed.

jokes.text: That, my friend, is "D Cup." How much have you won so far? -700. -Not 700 exactly? Double it.
jokes.setup: Joey continues winning at the fake card game
jokes.punchline: Chandler creates increasingly ridiculous rules like doubling at exactly $700
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the fake game with Chandler's commitment to absurd rules. 'D Cup' is a funny fake card term.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I'm on a roll here!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Okay, okay, new rule: you have to double down if you hit exactly $700.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What? That's insane!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh, and if you say the word 'card' you automatically lose half your money.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Chandler!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: And every third hand, you have to play backwards while hopping on one foot.

jokes.text: Don't get excited, that won't happen unless you get the.. No way!
jokes.setup: Chandler explains the final rule that would get Joey to exactly $1500
jokes.punchline: His fake shock when Joey 'miraculously' gets the right card
jokes.explanation: Chandler's performance of fake shock at the 'miraculous' outcome caps the elaborate scheme nicely.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Okay, so if you pick any card with a number on it that's divisible by three, you'll have exactly $1500.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh wow, really?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Okay, I'm picking a card.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Alright, let's see what you got.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: It's the three of diamonds.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh my God! You got exactly $1500! How did you do that?!

jokes.text: I was just gonna go across the hall and write that on Chandler.
jokes.setup: Monica labels Rachel's clothes box 'What were you thinking?'
jokes.punchline: Monica's threat to write the same insult on Chandler
jokes.explanation: Monica's petty threat to insult Chandler too shows her emotional state but isn't particularly clever.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: And if you don't start folding your clothes properly, I'm labeling your box 'What were you thinking?' too.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Hey, I fold my clothes!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No, you don't.

jokes.text: She has 147,000 pair of boots.
jokes.setup: Monica is forced to list Rachel's good roommate qualities
jokes.punchline: Her ridiculous exaggeration of Rachel's boot collection
jokes.explanation: Monica's exaggerated number is mildly funny but fairly standard hyperbole humor.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: She has... really great boots. Like, hundreds of them. Thousands maybe. I've seen her boot collection take up entire rooms. She has a separate apartment just for boots. Some say she's still discovering new boots in there.

jokes.text: And you stretch them out with your big old clown feet.
jokes.setup: Monica says Rachel lets her borrow boots
jokes.punchline: Rachel's insulting description of Monica's feet
jokes.explanation: 'Big old clown feet' is a decent insult but not particularly original or clever.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: You can borrow my boots, but Monica, honestly, your feet look like two brown raisins that someone stepped on.

jokes.text: That's nice. I like having things to read in the bathroom.
jokes.setup: Monica mentions Rachel leaves notes on the bathroom mirror
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's matter-of-fact response about bathroom reading material
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's practical take on the mirror notes is perfectly in character - she misses the emotional point entirely.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Well, I mean, what else are you gonna read in there?

jokes.text: Bring Monica, and we'll live together! We'll have so much fun!
jokes.setup: Phoebe is excited about living with Rachel
jokes.punchline: Her suggestion to bring Monica too for a three-way living situation
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's naive suggestion shows she doesn't understand the complexity but isn't particularly funny.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: We could all live together! You, me, and Monica! It would be like a three-way!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Phoebe, Monica already lives with Chandler.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: So? We could make it a four-way!

jokes.text: Kind of.
jokes.setup: Phoebe asks if Monica is still set on moving in with Chandler
jokes.punchline: Monica's understated response to this major life decision
jokes.explanation: Monica's casual response to moving in with Chandler is mildly amusing understatement but not strong.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Yeah, I'm pretty much set on it.

jokes.text: Get your head out of your shirt there, son!
jokes.setup: Ross is still pretending to have Ben with him
jokes.punchline: His command to his 'son' reveals he's using an inanimate object with a shirt
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of fake Ben reveal - Ross talking to what's apparently a pumpkin in a shirt is absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Ben, sit down. We need to talk about your mother.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: No, Ben, you can't have ice cream for dinner.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Because I said so, that's why.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Don't you take that tone with me, young man. You're grounded.

jokes.text: Yeah, it's a pumpkin. I'll come pack.
jokes.setup: Ross has been caught in his fake Ben charade
jokes.punchline: His matter-of-fact admission that he was using a pumpkin as fake Ben
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the fake Ben storyline. Ross's casual admission and immediate pivot to helping pack is excellent character comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Look, I was using a pumpkin, okay? It was round, it was orange, it worked!

jokes.text: Then I get a jack and a king. Boom! Ross gets a 4 and 5. Even Ross was getting the Cup card, the D Cup... the sitting-down bonus. I didn't even get half a cup! Nothing!
jokes.setup: Joey explains how he lost so badly at 'Cups'
jokes.punchline: His detailed recounting of the fake game terms shows he completely bought into it
jokes.explanation: Joey's complete absorption of the fake game terms like 'sitting-down bonus' shows perfect gullibility. Great character comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I was doing great! I had two cups stacked, and then Chandler said I needed to watch out for the 'Gnutz Gambit,' so I got all nervous and knocked over my whole pyramid!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Then he hit me with a 'Reverse Tenaka Blast' and I didn't even know what that was, so I just... I panicked!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I'm telling you, it's a real game. Very competitive. Very strategic.

jokes.text: I think beginner's luck is very important in Cups!
jokes.setup: Joey is confused about losing to Ross at the fake game
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's confident assertion about strategy in a made-up game
jokes.explanation: Phoebe treating the fake game as real and offering strategy advice is perfectly in character for her earnest weirdness.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: You have to keep your cards close to your chest. That's the key to Gidlet.

jokes.text: Wait a second. I see what you're trying to do here. You're trying to give me money again!
jokes.setup: Chandler offers to buy the white dog for $1500
jokes.punchline: Joey finally catches on to Chandler's money-giving scheme
jokes.explanation: Joey finally realizing Chandler's pattern of trying to help him financially. Good character moment but not exceptional.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait. You're buying me a dog for fifteen hundred dollars?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, I thought you'd like it.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: But why would you do that?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Because you're my friend and I want to help you out.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh my God. OH MY GOD! You've been giving me money this whole time!

jokes.text: And now the dog.
jokes.setup: Joey says he'll miss Chandler
jokes.punchline: His addition that he'll also miss the dog he just gave away
jokes.explanation: Joey's emotional addition about missing the dog he just gave up is touching and funny. Good economy and character work.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I'll miss you, Chandler.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: And I'll miss that dog I just gave away.

jokes.text: What the hell is that dog doing here?
jokes.setup: Chandler and Monica are in their new apartment
jokes.punchline: The white dog has mysteriously appeared in their new place
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing the dog somehow made it to their new apartment. Sets up the final Cups revelation.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Monica, where did this dog come from?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I have no idea! It was just here when I came in!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, I didn't bring it. Although I have to say, it's better than the last surprise that showed up in our apartment.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What are you talking about?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: You. On our first night.

jokes.text: What kind of idiot do you take me for? It's not a real game. I made it up.
jokes.setup: Chandler wants his money back from Joey for the dog
jokes.punchline: Chandler's exasperated admission that Cups isn't real
jokes.explanation: Chandler's frustrated confession that he made up Cups is a good revelation moment, though somewhat expected.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Okay, fine! You don't have to give me the money back. But I want the dog.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You can't have the dog, Chandler. Cups is my best friend.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Joey, Cups isn't real!

jokes.text: I'm sorry you lost your money. But I won it fair and square. At a fake game! If you wanna try to make some of it back... I'd be glad to play you for it. But I should warn you. I am very good at Cups.
jokes.setup: Chandler reveals Cups is fake
jokes.punchline: Joey's confidence that he's good at the fake game and willingness to play Chandler
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - even knowing it's fake, he's confident in his Cups ability and willing to play the inventor. Excellent character comedy that shows Joey's logic.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Cups is fake.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What? No it's not!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yes it is. I made it up.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Well, I'm really good at it anyway.

jokes.text: I assume the saucer card came up when you played last. Let's see if it comes up this time.
jokes.setup: Joey is about to play Cups against its inventor
jokes.punchline: Joey confidently references another made-up card term
jokes.explanation: Joey's addition of 'saucer card' to the Cups mythology while playing against Chandler is perfect. Shows he's internalized and expanded the fake game.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I know all about Cups. I've been playing Cups since I was a kid. In fact, I'm pretty sure I invented a move called the Cups Flush.

tmdb_overview: On the last night before Chandler and Monica move in together, Monica and Rachel argue, while Chandler tries to give Joey money to help him pay the upcoming bills.
insight: Quieter farewell setup leans on character beats over rapid-fire laughs, averaging 3.4 jokes per minute.
